channel.title: 2026-03-17 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-17 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: [ASAP] Toward Reaction Vessel Mimicry: Machine Learning-Assisted Automated Exploration of Alkene Polymerization and Its Transferability
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Sagar Ghorai, Ruben Staub, Yu Harabuchi, Takashi Nakano, Alexandre Varnek, and Satoshi Maeda
Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation DOI: 10.1021/acs.jctc.5c02120

item.title: Constructing machine learning interatomic potentials with minimum amount of ab initio data

npj Computational Materials, Published online: 17 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02023-y Constructing machine learning interatomic potentials with minimum amount of ab initio data

item.title: 50-km Fiber Interferometer for Testing Gravitational Signatures in Quantum Interference
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Haocun Yu, Dorotea Macri, Thomas Morling, Eleonora Polini, Thomas B. Mieling, Peter Barrow, Begüm Kabagöz, Xinghui Yin, Piotr T. Chruściel, Christopher Hilweg, Eric Oelker, Nergis Mavalvala, and Philip Walther
Author(s): Haocun Yu, Dorotea Macri, Thomas Morling, Eleonora Polini, Thomas B. Mieling, Peter Barrow, Begüm Kabagöz, Xinghui Yin, Piotr T. Chruściel, Christopher Hilweg, Eric Oelker, Nergis Mavalvala, and Philip Walther Quantum mechanics and general relativity are the foundational pillars of modern physics, yet experimental tests that combine the two frameworks remain rare. Measuring optical phase shifts of massless photons in a gravitational potential provides a unique quantum platform to probe gravity beyond Newt… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 110803] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: bifrost : A first-principles model of polarization mode dispersion in optical fiber
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Patrick R. Banner, S. L. Rolston, and Joseph W. Britton
Author(s): Patrick R. Banner, S. L. Rolston, and Joseph W. Britton Birefringence in optical fiber causes polarization mode dispersion (PMD), which can broaden telecommunication signals, degrade fiber-sensor measurements, and scramble polarization-encoded quantum states. Though widely studied, PMD is typically modeled using statistical descriptions that obscure its underlying physical origins. The authors present BIFROST, a first-principles model that links PMD to specific physical parameters such as core geometry, temperature, and bend radius. Using this model, they simulate the impact of environmental variations on PMD compensation and demonstrate how knowledge of fiber properties ( e.g. fiber spinning) can be applied to emerging quantum networks. [Phys. Rev. Applied 25, 034054] Publishe…

item.title: Continuous cloud position spectroscopy using a magneto-optical trap
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Benedikt Heizenreder, Ananya Sitaram, Sana Boughdachi, Andrew von Hörsten, Yan Xie, Andreas Brodschelm, and Florian Schreck
Author(s): Benedikt Heizenreder, Ananya Sitaram, Sana Boughdachi, Andrew von Hörsten, Yan Xie, Andreas Brodschelm, and Florian Schreck Precision frequency references are essential for quantum technologies and navigation systems, but stabilization methods are often constrained by narrow locking ranges and noise sensitivity, which thwart long-term stability and operation outside a laboratory. The authors use continuous spectroscopy with the vertical position of a magneto-optical trap as a frequency reference, to achieve resolution 30 times below the natural transition linewidth and expand locking range by orders of magnitude. Their direct extraction of optical and rf references with superior long-term stability offers a practical route toward robust references for quantum technology and pot…

item.title: Dielectric properties of single-crystal calcium tungstate
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Elrina Hartman, Michael E. Tobar, Ben T. McAllister, Jeremy Bourhill, Andreas Erb, and Maxim Goryachev
Author(s): Elrina Hartman, Michael E. Tobar, Ben T. McAllister, Jeremy Bourhill, Andreas Erb, and Maxim Goryachev This investigation employed microwave whispering-gallery mode (WGM) analysis to characterize the dielectric properties of a cylindrical, single-crystal sample of calcium tungstate (CaWO₄). Through investigation of quasi-transverse-magnetic and quasi-transverse-electric mode … [Phys. Rev. Applied 25, 034055] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Goldstone-Mediated Polar Instability in Hexagonal Barium Titanate
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Struan Simpson, Urmimala Dey, Robin Sjökvist, Jonathan Wright, Clemens Ritter, Richard Beanland, Nicholas C. Bristowe, and Mark S. Senn
Author(s): Struan Simpson, Urmimala Dey, Robin Sjökvist, Jonathan Wright, Clemens Ritter, Richard Beanland, Nicholas C. Bristowe, and Mark S. Senn We discover a rare structural manifestation of the Goldstone paradigm in a hexagonal polytype of the prototypical ferroelectric BaTiO₃. First-principles calculations confirm the Goldstone character of the order parameter, while our high-resolution diffraction measurements unveil an u… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116101] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Impact of leakage on the dynamics of an ST₀ qubit implemented in a double quantum dot device
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Javier Oliva del Moral, Olatz Sanz Larrarte, Reza Dastbasteh, Josu Etxezarreta Martinez, and Rubén M. Otxoa
Author(s): Javier Oliva del Moral, Olatz Sanz Larrarte, Reza Dastbasteh, Josu Etxezarreta Martinez, and Rubén M. Otxoa Spin qubits in quantum dots are a promising technology for quantum computing due to their fast response time and long coherence times. An electromagnetic pulse is applied to the system for a specific duration to perform a desired rotation. To avoid decoherence, the amplitude and gate time must be hi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 094310] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Lattice vacancy migration barriers in Fe-Ni alloys, and an indication as to why Ni atoms diffuse slowly: A first-principles study
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Adam M. Fisher, Christopher D. Woodgate, Xiaoyu Zhang, George C. Hadjipanayis, Laura H. Lewis, and Julie B. Staunton
Author(s): Adam M. Fisher, Christopher D. Woodgate, Xiaoyu Zhang, George C. Hadjipanayis, Laura H. Lewis, and Julie B. Staunton Lattice vacancy migration barriers in ferromagnetic FeₓNi₁−ₓ alloys (0.4≤x≤0.6) are accurately quantified within the framework of ab initio electronic structure calculations using the nudged elastic band (NEB) method. Both the atomically disordered (A1) fcc phase, as … [Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 034410] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Multistep transitions in noncollinear spin zigzag chain in BaCoTe₂O₇
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Dequan Jiang, Guiling Xiao, Huakun Zuo, Zhuo Zeng, Hao Huang, Youyuan Liang, Shanshan Pan, Tianyi Li, Meng Wang, Zhongwen Ouyang, and Zhengcai Xia
Author(s): Dequan Jiang, Guiling Xiao, Huakun Zuo, Zhuo Zeng, Hao Huang, Youyuan Liang, Shanshan Pan, Tianyi Li, Meng Wang, Zhongwen Ouyang, and Zhengcai Xia The physical properties of BaCoTe₂O₇ single crystals were investigated using magnetization, magnetostriction, electron spin resonance, and electric polarization under static and pulsed high magnetic fields. Experimental results reveal that single-crystal sample… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 094436] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Proximity Induced Magnetic Anisotropy and Trefoil Fermiology in Monolayer FeCl2/Bi(111)
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Shigemi Terakawa, Binbin Liu, Frank Schindler, Titus Neupert, Jing‐Rong Ji, Gabriele Domaine, Emily C. McFarlane, Daiyu Geng, Jiabao Yang, Fadi Choueikani, Philippe Ohresser, Manuel Valvidares, Pierluigi Gargiani, Craig Polley, Gerardina Carbone, Mats Leandersson, Stuart S. P. Parkin, Amilcar Bedoya‐Pinto, Niels B. M. Schröter
Advanced Materials, Volume 38, Issue 16, 17 March 2026.
